specific_mechanism: Optical kerning: algorithm computes per-pair letter spacing from glyph outlines rather than from a fixed kern-pair table. Pair-by-pair adjustment travels the null-space of {monospace, ugly, illegible} to land on optimal aesthetic readability.
llm_application: Optical-kerning tokenizer: rather than a fixed BPE merge table, dynamically adjust the merge boundary between two adjacent subword tokens based on the local context's outline (embedding similarity, attention attention-pattern). Null-space traversal: the set of legal tokenizations forms a null-space; pick the one that maximizes downstream task utility per pass.
novelty_claim: SuperBPE and BoundlessBPE (COLM 2025) relax pre-tokenization boundaries, but they still freeze the merge order. Whether 'context-dependent re-tokenization per inference step' is in 2024-2026 print is the open question.
